howItWorks.intro: This torque wrench setting calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.

rules.name: Corrected Torque
rules.explanation: Adjust the dial to compensate for the increased lever arm.

howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: Do extensions change torque readings?
faq.answer: Yes, adding a crow foot or extension changes the effective lever arm, requiring a corrected dial setting.

faq.question: When do I need to adjust?
faq.answer: Only when the extension adds length at an angle to the torque wrench handle.

faq.question: Is the Torque Wrench Setting Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard practical calculation formulas trusted by users. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

faq.question: What target torque should I enter?
faq.answer: Enter the most accurate target torque value you have available. If you're estimating, use a conservative figure. You can always run the calculator again with different values to see how changes affect the results.

sections.title: What Is the Torque Wrench Setting Calculator?
sections.body: The Torque Wrench Setting Calculator is a free online tool designed for users who need quick, accurate calculations in the practical calculation space. By entering your target torque, wrench length, extension length, you get instant results including dial setting, applied force at handle. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting dial setting right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In practical calculation, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by users worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to get accurate results with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your target torque and need to find the right dial setting. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Target Torque (ft-lbs)
howToSteps.description: Type or select your target torque in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Wrench Length (inches)
howToSteps.description: Type or select your wrench length in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Extension Length (inches)
howToSteps.description: Type or select your extension length in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your dial setting, applied force at handle. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your target torque before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both dial setting and applied force at handle — they work together to give you the full picture.
